brand.name: Jeenie Jolie
brand.tagline: Organic Beauty, and Mindful Living, Wrapped In Art

offer.what: Organic, artisanal skincare and body care products — tallow-based face cream, whipped body butters, body oils, African black soap, shea butter, sugar scrubs, and natural loofahs. Bundles available. Lunar ceremony ritual content/kits referenced but not a structured paid service.
offer.coreOutcome: Glowing, nourished skin through clean, intentional beauty rituals — feeling confident and aligned through slow, mindful self-care.
offer.pricePoint: $12.99–$44.99 individual products; ~$80.94 bundles. One-time purchases. No subscription tier found.
offer.commitment: One-off purchase; no recurring billing observed.
leadMagnets: 30% off first order via email signup (code HAPPY30)
Free guided ritual kit download for lunar ceremonies (referenced on homepage)

audience.icp: Women aged 25–45 interested in clean/organic beauty, spiritual self-care, and intentional slow-living. Values natural ingredients, mystical aesthetics, and products that feel like rituals rather than routines.
painPoints: Frustrated by synthetic chemicals and harsh ingredients in mainstream skincare
Seeking beauty products that align with a spiritual or wellness lifestyle
Wants skincare that actually delivers results without compromising on clean ingredients
Desires a sense of ritual and meaning in daily self-care — not just function
segments: Clean-beauty first-timers — curious about natural/organic but haven't fully switched
Spiritual/wellness devotees — moon ritual practitioners, intentional living community
Gift buyers — purchasing artisanal bundles as gifts for wellness-oriented friends/family

salesCycle.length: Same day to a few days (e-commerce impulse + consideration cycle)
salesCycle.trial: No free trial. 30% off first order is the primary conversion incentive.
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Self-serve add-to-cart on Shopify storefront. Email discount code drives first purchase.
salesCycle.topObjection: Skepticism about tallow-based or natural formulas vs. familiar drugstore products; price comparison to mass-market alternatives.

ahaMoment: First use of the product — experiencing the texture, scent, and skin-feel of a tallow cream or whipped body butter and noticing it performs better than conventional alternatives.

retention.recurring: No subscription or membership model detected. Repeat purchase is the only revenue recurrence.
retention.expansion: Buying additional SKUs (e.g., adding body butter after trying face cream); purchasing bundles; gifting.
retention.churnRisk: One-and-done buyers who don't receive a post-purchase nurture sequence encouraging a second order or cross-sell.

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding — likely only a Shopify order confirmation; no brand story or ritual-use education sequence
Lead-Magnet Nurture — 30% code likely delivered in one email with no follow-up if not redeemed
Trial/Consult Conversion — N/A (no trial product), but no browse-abandonment or cart-abandonment flow evident
Value Newsletter — no regular educational or community-building content cadence observed
Repeat-Purchase / Expansion — no cross-sell or replenishment email flow detected
Win-Back / Re-Engagement — no lapsed-customer reactivation sequence evident
Referral / Advocacy — no referral program or UGC encouragement flow found
toneVoice: Mystical, poetic, and aspirational. Blends wellness vocabulary ('vibration,' 'intention,' 'ritual') with empowering self-sovereignty language. Warm and introspective, never salesy or clinical. Reads like a journal entry from a confident, spiritual woman.
compliance: Standard e-commerce (CAN-SPAM / GDPR). No HIPAA, financial, or legal constraints. Skincare claims should avoid drug-claim language (FDA cosmetic rules).
